name: Retune the tensor tile so each warp does more MMA work per shared-memory feed

summary.name: Retune the tensor tile so each warp does more MMA work per shared-memory feed
summary.hypothesis: The 32x32 CTA uses only 2 warps and each warp consumes 1 A fragment plus 2 B fragments for just 2 MMA ops per K-slice. After the 16-byte async-copy change, global delivery improved, but the kernel is still dominated by shared-memory issue pressure (`smsp__warp_issue_stalled_mio_throttle_per_warp_active.pct = 42.83`) and weak residency (`sm__warps_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active = 45.70`). A tile retune that increases math per warp, starting with a wider N footprint and only then adding more warps if registers stay reasonable, should convert the faster staging path into higher tensor utilization.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Shared-memory / fragment-load issue pressure with too little MMA work per warp and too few ready warps to hide it.
summary.risk: Moderate. More output tiles per warp or a larger CTA can raise accumulator pressure, registers, and shared-memory footprint enough to erase the gain if occupancy collapses or the store path becomes awkward.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the first tile variant pushes registers or shared memory high enough that active warps stay near 45.7% and runtime does not improve, or if `mio_throttle` remains roughly flat while occupancy gets worse.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
